letters.title: To Someone I Could Never Forget
letters.content:
There are words that arrive too late, yet somehow find their way home. This is one of them.

I've spent countless nights thinking about what I would say if I had the chance. How do you capture something so vast in mere sentences? How do you explain a feeling that has no name?

You were the kind of person who made the ordinary feel magical. A simple coffee became an adventure. A quiet moment became a memory worth keeping forever.

I should have told you then. I should have been brave enough to say the things my heart was screaming. Instead, I kept silent, convinced that the right time would come. It never did.

But perhaps this is meant for you now. Perhaps some things are worth saying even if they arrive late. You changed me. You taught me what it means to feel deeply, to love without reservation, to hold someone's memory like a sacred thing.

Thank you for the moments we shared. Thank you for reminding me what it means to be alive.

Some feelings stay even after the words end.

letters.title: The Garden We Never Built
letters.content:
Do you remember that day we sat on your porch and talked about the future? We had plans. Such beautiful, impossible plans.

We were going to build a garden together. You said you wanted roses—deep red ones that smelled like old love songs. I said I'd plant jasmine so the night would always smell like dreams.

We never built that garden. Life happened. Time did what time always does—it moved on, taking us to different places, different lives.

But I still think about it sometimes. I still think about those roses and jasmine that exist only in our conversations, blooming in a garden that only we can see.

This letter is to tell you that those flowers were real. That dream mattered. You mattered.

Somewhere in the universe, in some version of the world where time moved differently, that garden exists. And it's beautiful because it was ours.

letters.title: November Rain
letters.content:
I'm writing this on a rainy November evening, watching the droplets race down the window. I wonder if it's raining where you are too.

November always reminds me of you. Maybe it's because you were born in this month, or maybe it's because November itself feels like a goodbye—the last breath of autumn before winter takes over.

I saw something today that made me think of you. A old bookstore, the kind you loved. The kind with creaky wooden floors and the smell of aged paper and forgotten dreams. I wanted to call you. I almost did.

But some calls shouldn't be made. Some silences are more honest than words.

I hope you're well. I hope you found what you were looking for. I hope November brings you warmth, even if it always feels like a season of goodbyes to me.

Stay warm. Stay loved. Stay remembered.
